play_store_reviews.text: Nice idea, but this damn app can't count! I keep checking off tasks, and some don't show up in the Daily total, no matter how much I make sure that I'm online. So, DO NOT TRUST it until they enable the app to count your tasks fully OFFLINE! How many weekly successes have I been cheated out of, because finished tasks don't count?? Update: Yeah, I contacted support; days later, still waiting; time to migrate to a less bloated app that doesn't depend on Web access.

I think I tried Todoist years ago and forgot about it, but it's stuck with me this time around. The free version is excellent; the only feature I'm really missing is the ability to add deadlines, which I can live without, at least for now. I love the calendar integration and the widget. There are zero ads which is amazing. Thanks Todoist for helping me with productivity!

I love how it has connections to gmail and google calendar, and how it has a web view in addition to a phone app and a desktop app. It's incredible how much they give you with the free version (the free version is plenty for my needs), and it has no ads! One of my favorite features is the AI ability to just type the due date in the name of the task. The one thing I would really like changed is if emails attached to tasks were stored within Todoist somehow, so I am free to delete them from gmail.

Absolutely loved this app and have been a subscriber since May 2021 or 2022. However, after the notice of price increase from $48/yr to $60‽ Cancelled my subscription, and I'll just have to find something else. The app is super great, but financially it was already expensive and to increase the price in these times‽ Yeah no, I'm disappointed in the company.

Caution to anyone who signs up for a free trial of premium. I found it quite difficult to find how to cancel the trial before I got charged. then they force you to actually type feedback before you can cancel. Just don't want anyone to get tricked into paying for a subscription they don't want because a company makes it intentionally difficult to cancel.

this app has been great for keeping track of what I have to do each day. it conveniently syncs on all of my devices so it's always up to date no matter where I am and what device I'm using. it's easy and satisfying to mark something as "complete". it reminds you when you have overdue tasks, but gives you the option to quickly reschedule them to another time so you dont feel guilty. all in all, it's a great app that does exactly what it needs to do! highly recommended!

Fantastic. I don't just use it every day, I rely on it every day. Perhaps the best thing I can point out in a short review: the power of knowing I have to do something at a future date, telling ToDoist to put it on the list for then, and letting go of the weight of 'what if I forget?'. Tasks can be set for 'every other Tuesday' or 'every! 30 days'- i.e. 30 days from the last completion - great for filter changes or the like. I can create tasks via email and Teams with ease. Again: fantastic.

What is the point of a "To Do" list without reminders? Nope, you don't get a notification reminder unless you pay for premium, you can't even try it to see if it will work better than Microsoft To-Do (which can't stay running for anything). Without notifications/alarms I can make a "To Do" list using a notebook & pen or the paper calendar I have on my fridge! The whole point of searching for a useful app is to use modern features, like reminders/alarms & custom days, dates, & times.

Clunky for my purposes. Seemed like a good reminder app, I needed a list app. I wanted something I could use to save a grocery list, not something that tells me "Hey it's Tuesday time to go to the grocery." There's not a way to do that here without navigating too many menus. I'm coming from Wunderlist, so I am used to having direct and clean access to a group of to-do lists. Todoist hides your lists in favor of showing you upcoming tasks and reminders. I already have a calendar for that.

I am a fan of GTD which is really just a system of lists. I have tried all the big name task managers out there. Todoist is by far my favorite. Once you catch on to the natural language processing it is so easy to record everything that comes to mind in the "In" box then do a review later to decide what to do. Since I always have my phone I use the notification bar icon to record stuff as I think of it. Nothing ever gets lost or forgotten. It is wondeful to have a septem you can depend on!

Seems like a good tool, but almost nothing that I actually need to do with it is possible without a subscription. I'd happily puchase the app, or do a free trial period, but honestly, it annoys me when companies offer a free download only to put everything useful behind a paywall, especially when they try to give a discount for a year's sub before you even really get to try the features. Nope.

Over time I've tried a lot of to-do list apps. Todoist has long been one I've come back to over and over and finally, I've settled on it as my favorite. During the past few years Todoist has helped me stay focused to grow my freelance business. It's also helped me when I get anxious and overwhelmed. I can easily set my daily list to just a few manageable tasks and move things to the back burner or "inbox". I love Todoist and its Getting Things Done methodology.

You won't be able to label, comment (add more details), or get reminders on the free version, but that makes the free version pretty fair. You'll be able to sort things by project and date and prioritize things, and that makes it easy to see what you're going to do from day to day. This is all in a smooth, ad free experience. Plus you get a nice notification every morning telling you what tasks are due that day.

downloaded. I spent 10 min on it. deleted. wanted an app I could speak into and set tasks, dates, and reminders. I currently have voice notes, which works awesome for the first 2. It doesn't do reminders, unfortunately, so I need to constantly go back in and check the list. I drive a lot, so hands-free is essential. We have the technology, let's use it.

Great app. I pay for the subscription because I find the app invaluable for creating structure in my life. However, there was a recent update that changed how reminders are set, and while I do like the new feature, I wish they didn't remove the old one that allowed you to type a specific reminder with your keyboard. For example, I used to be able to type in something like, "every other Sunday at 8am", but now I can only select a specific date and time unless I use Todoist on my PC.

Fantastic tool. Keeps all my tasks beautifully organized. Bug free. Wish that it was easier to manage recurrance. Seems you can only manage recurrance using the text feature in the name, but if you want to change the date or time of an occurrence you lose the recurrance settings. I wish there were an option for manually setting up the recurrence settings.

Very good to do list app! Looks nice, easy to create new tasks with the automatic detection of dates, times, priorities, and topic. Does not show ads and is pretty generous with the free version. My only issue so far has been occasional desync between devices (can be solved by logging out and logging back in). Also, note that the free version doesn't allow you to set reminders.

Awesome but not perfect. Best I've found so far, and I've tried a ton. With a lot of my workflow being email/outlook based, the plug-in is incredibly helpful. It's a little complex, but with great power comes a bit of learning curve. I have an Android phone, an iPad, and work in Windows so the cross platform nature is key for me. Missing a couple things I think are key like being able to enter a completed date in the past (yesterday at least). I'm getting far more done using the app.

Powerful and simple. The only thing missing is integration with Google calendar, which I used for years, and I do not see any option to migrate either. So, no backward search for the events, calendar sharing and other integrations. Well, task and project-wise, this is a powerhouse! Much more simple and well designed than Google Keep I've been using for years.

I rely on this app daily for all my personal to do lists. But recent changes to functionality have made it less friendly to use than when I first got it.  For example it's much harder to add a time. Whenever I add a time to a task, it assumes I want to do it today, and I have to go back in and change it back to the original day.   Another annoyance is I can never use the word "march" in a task (like the March for Science) because the app thinks I mean the month. There are other words that happens with too, I can't recall them all right now.  Also like another use I don't like the circles on the left to check off the tasks, I keep deleting them accidentally.

Best to do list I've found. I used to use Trello but it didn't work for me. This does everything Trello didn't. Love the Inbox and how i can access it from a widget on my phone with one press. Also i have the other widget that shows my list of choice. I love how you can schedule items just by typing when you want them, even if it repeats. It's a very smooth interface. A few of the premium features would be really nice to have. It's tempting but i don't like recurring fees. I'd rather pay once.

Seems like a pretty cool app, I like how quick it is to set up tasks. Would be great for some people's situations, but since I'm looking for something for personal use and have no need for additional projects, larger files (or files in general), or filters it is a waste of money to pay a monthly subscription just to be able to set up any reminders at all. Guess I'm back to my search for a good todo list app...

I recently moved from a job where I utilized Microsoft Outlook's tasks. I struggled to find an app that could perform as well as Outlook. This app does everything and more! On PC...I love the predictive typing, Google Chrome attachment, flagging emails, typing notes, attaching files, reminders, and more. And it syncs across all my devices. On the app I love how fluid it is. It is so easy to use! After trying literally almost every to do app out there, this one gets my five stars! I have tried Google Tasks, Microsoft tasks, Wunderlist, Any.do, and many more. I've even paid for a majority of them. This one is well worth the money!

I love this app! I've tried a few other task list type applications, but this one really takes the cake! Their format is very flexible and has all the basics offered for free...except reminders. Data syncs well across devices, you can create subprojects, stacked task lists, and many other useful features! I check Todoist throughout my day and it's working well for me so far. If you need reminders, check out their premium version! It has plenty of features that are actually worth paying for. ;)

A few days in and so far so good. Definitely has a few non-intuitive functions. Setting custom reminders is weird...a pop-up should just appear to select a date and time, but instead you type it in then it tries to interpret what you type, then you select it if it is right. Very convoluted. Finally found the task completion statistics. Its next to your profile icon/picture when clicking on the three horizontal lines top left. Small circle with squiggly arrow with some numbers next to it.

The app is missing some very simple features. For example, once I sort by priority, I cannot sort anymore. I can't drag one P1 priority over another. The app is missing the ability to add new tasks to the top of your list. For example, if my list contains 30 tasks and a new task comes in that I need to do next, it gets thrown to the bottom of the list. The cost is also a bit weird considering it's lacking features that cheaper apps include.

Functions fine in general, easy drag to subtasks. Can't adjust the Notifications to not go through "Do not disturb" mode (bad if you work off shifts), can't set timing of notifications. In a productivity app, scheduling is important. Reminders require premium, seriously? I'll just install one of the tons of free apps that include it for free rather than pay $3 month. Overall: Average functioning app that requires paid premium for some basic functions.

This app has everything I was looking for, but a bit confusing. At 9 tasks on the free version, it wouldn't let me add anymore. There was no indication why, it just wouldn't add anything. It's not enough tasks to use this as a daily planner. Plus, there wasn't anything that said about a limit or if buying pro would boost that limit. I'm too poor to take that chance. Bummer

I really like this app and use it everyday. However, these new Pop Ups that come out of nowhere congratulating me on checking off tasks are incredibly annoying. I don't need a digital pat on the back. Maybe some people like that and that's great. Personally, I just want to keep checking off tasks or looking at what's next to do and these pop ups just get in the way of that and irritate me. Can you please make it so I can turn them off? I've searched the settings and I can't find it anywhere.

I love the app. I just wish there was an option for a one time payment for premium. I have too many subscriptions, and I always cancel new subscriptions in a short amount of time because I find that I don't use the app enough to pay monthly or yearly. The apps that I can pay once for premium are the apps I usually stick with. I use this a lot for tracking my spending and budget, so a subscription is kind of counter intuitive.

I use this app everyday at work--it's a great app with lots of options for keeping tasks organized. However, the latest update seems to have introduced a bug. Sometimes, while adding sub-tasks to a task, the app will auto-complete everything. All sub-tasks will simultaneously mark themselves as completed, and the only way to undo it is to go back and uncheck them one by one. Sometimes the cursor will also skip to the "Comments" box while in the middle of writing a sub-task.

The ability to prioritize and assign dates has been awesome in two major ways. First, the underlying stress of not knowing if I'm missing something is way down. I've kept other to-do lists, but they get so bloated and it's easy to lose track of what is really important, and then the very process of going through to check became a job in itself. The second way this app has changed my life is professionally. I answer emails quickly and finish easy things I need to do for teammates first.

Easy to use. It takes a second to get used to controls but then it super easy. I use the widget to add task throughout the day. My list stays condensed in my notifications. Only when I click on it, does it show me everything on my list. I love this app because it helps me remember things, and helps me stay on track when I have a million things going at once (I'm a Mom, Wife and Student).

After trying *so* many different time management apps (even the $200/yr ones), this one the best. I love that the date you type in the add task bar automatically assigns it to the date. You can set events, tasks, and reminders all with voice to text while driving! The only thing I'd *love* is a more conventional "calendar" view where I can easily see what's in store for the month with one glance. Keep up the good work!

I've tried multiple to-do list organizer applications and have found Todoist to be the best for me. Sync was a big problem cross platform for me even using Microsoft's To-Do. I'd create my lists ans tasks on my PC and then go to pull them up on my phone when on the job site onky to find nothing I had entered was there. This happened time and time again. so began my search and I found Todoist. It is fast, easy to organize. You can tag, prioritize, add photos. I really like the karma too!

I downloaded this app because it's one that syncs with our smart speaker. However, I have recurring tasks, some of which are daily and that option does not work at all. It works are far as setting them up goes. Once I mark a task as done it skips the next due date. So my daily tasks are only showing up every other day. Not happy with that at all. I am in the process of finding another app ASAP!

Since my first rating I have used Todoist for years. It is easy and powerful. It can be used in combination with Outlook, the email I must use at work. You can jot down quick reminders or organize complex projects or even keep detailed organized notes. You can set up filters to help you focus your work, which is very helpful. I love it and can't imagine using anything else.

Would be better if the Inbox was not treated as a project, but rather a scheduling location, and that items could be created in the inbox and EASILY (bulk) moved to other projects (but stay in the inbox) to then be scheduled to daily or next week or whatever Tracking would be easier. and the app would get a higher rating and I may actually subscribe.. Its a deal breaker for me.

Just tried to use it. It showed some buttons during set up, including how to use Todoist. Turned it on, clicked on the sentence, finally continued on. I searched around, but never found help using the app. This might be a great app for people that know how to use it or are seasoned enough in apps to figure it out, but wherever the guidance is for those that don't know, it's buried too deeply to be any help. I was unable to do anything useful and, instead, a big waste of a chunk of my morning!

I've been using this app for years, but suddently it is giving me emails warning that my account will be deleted because I don't use it which is a bit frustrating. Also they've changed how many projects you can have unless you pay $5 per month. Before this change I had 12, now I can only have 5. Very dissapointing. I do like that there are limited ads and that I can get notifications in my email, I just wish there was a flat fee instead of a monthly fee for extras.

This app is easy to learn and use, and yet so powerful. Update June 1st 2024: well I tried upgrading to the pro version today so that I could get reminders. Setting a reminder didn't work for me at all. First of all The quick selections for reminder time and date were shown to be relative to today. Yet the task I wanted to be reminded about is a year away! That just seems so silly. Of course reminders should default to be near the time of the task! Then no matter how hard I tried I couldn't save

It's a great app, but just like any organization app, you have to meet it halfway with actual effort. If you don't actually intend to complete the work, don't kid yourself by getting the app. I only wish it included a calendar that displayed events in chronological order along with the list of events in bullet point format and that it sent reminders of events. I'm very picky about apps, but this one is actually nice.

I love this app and I count on it to keep me organized but am not crazy about the latest update. I love that my list appears larger and easier to read but I don't like how it automatically deletes it when an item is hit. I've accidently lost items on my grocery list several times. It has left me feeling less secure that I've crossed everything off my list.

App generally works, but it's impossible to include any time or date information in a task name, which is incredibly frustrating. For example, if you want a task that says 'tell family I need the car tomorrow', you're out of luck because 'tomorrow' is not allowed. You can't 'prepare for meeting at 12' either, or any such thing. Very annoying system.

I tried the free version and was not impressed due to many features hiding behind the pay wall. Then I decided to try the paid version. Todoist pro does everything and more than any other program I have ever used. I strongly suggest trying the paid version. The customization can give you whatever you need in a Todo app! I can email to a task list even from mobile email. The ability to reference one task in another is a great way to link things together.

So far, it's great. Just a small suggestion though: In "comments section" of tasks, if there are multiple images posted, and I click one to view, I should then be able to view the next one by swiping left/right. Instead I have to click off the image I'm currently on and scroll down the list of comments to find the next one. This was not intuitive to me, because in most SMS apps, whenever you view an image someone has sent you, if you swipe to the left/right, it will scroll through all images.

Love this app! started with the free version and upgraded right away. It really helped me get my life and schedule under control when I started my business. I am still learning some of the more advanced ways to use it and find it gets more and more helpful with time. I love that i can send emails directly to it and make them into tasks and also i totally get into tracking my progress and getting points! im a geek like that LoL

do you know a location-based reminders, and the app is reminding me that location is turned off, I don't care I don't want to be reminded, make it stop!! Google rules using calendar appointment, the rules require permission may be denied until an event is requested., I'm not requesting events. I don't have any location based reminders, I don't think I've made any. I just applied today's update and I see no indication looking at the reminders that any one of them is location based.

This app has helped me re-establish good habits, remember things when I couldn't afford to forget (very forgetful person) and conquer my procrastination bit by bit. The slightly gamified interface helps to make the app fun, and more personalized for me. I do wish it were a little more like a game, some level-ups and rewards wouldn't hurt. Overall, very useful and encouraging. Doesn't feel clingy or noisy.

The app looked very promising, but there were too many "Upgrade to premium" notifications when initially clicking through the app. From what I saw, I probably would have paid for a year if I was able to try out the fill app first. Instead, I got what felt like being kicked in the face by upgrade notifications. For these reasons, 3 stars and lost buisness.

Been using the app every day for years to track everything I need to get done from day to day. One of my favorite apps. It's likely my #1 most used app. Easy to use. Update: Since the radio buttons were added to mark tasks as complete, Im findind that sometimes tasks are accidentally being remived from my list. Not a fan of the radio buttons! - too easy to accidentally click in them.

There are so many to do apps out there and so many comparison articles it was difficult to even choose one. I tried several and this one definitely seemed like the best so I purchased the premium version and it is great. Save yourself some time and aggravation just get this one. Try the free version first. The premium cost is $3 per month subscription bille yearly for $36 and although there is no free trial of the premium version you do have 30 days for a refund if you don't like it but you will.

Truly my favorite to-do list app I've ever tried. My one small quibble is that it always has trouble accepting multiple keywords, for example if it's Monday and I need to set up an items that is weekly on Tuesdays, I can't just type "Tuesday weekly" but have to go tinker around in the settings. I'd also like more customization over the look, with fonts and colors.

I have a notes app built into my phone that I use. I researched and found an article where someone tried 4 different to do list options. This one sounded best to me, as most people, like me, usually have their phone right there. But my notes app also has where I can put things in the order I'd like to do them. If you can add that feature, I'll try again.

It does what it needs to. I just wish instead of email notifications when things were over due that I would get push notifications instead. I think it is hard to balance having a free tool and a paid version for something like this and the free version gives you the bare minimum. I dont know how the paid version would provide me with any better service as an individual than the free version aside from push notifications.

One of the best personal or business task management apps for mobile or desktop use. Paid version is inexpensive for the value. Extensive integrations through such platforms as IFTTT. Very versatile. Very easy to use. While I regularly evaluate most others on the market, this is among the top five. And for the last 4 months, it's my go-to app for personal and various small businesses with up to five other collaborators. A simple calendar within the app is the only feature missing.

Incredibly convenient and simple, but I hope that the feature to attach photos (from your gallery or straight from your camera), links, and documents can be without the premium. Just a really naive wish, coming from a student. Other than that,  this app is really good, and its customer service is as admirable. Good job!

I have been on a mission to find a to-do list that syncs well between my phone, smartwatch, and google calendar. I have tried just about every to-do list out there and in some aspect, every single one of them fell short. I was about to give up hope when I came upon this app! Thanks to this app, I am now the organized and productive person that I always knew I could be but never could find the right tool to make it happen!! Thank you!!! ❤️

I've used this 2018. It's the best free to-do list app on the market. Sometimes they update and it sucks, but they are quick to listen and make course-corrections. The app has tons of extras that you can use, some free, some paid. But I'm a simple guy who wants a simple to-do list app and this does it for me. 5 stars for the consistency, simplicity, and for listening to their customers when they make a mistake.

New interface 1 star for # signs instead of the clean easy to read circles. 🤬 Previous 5 Star review: I'm an Enlightepaid todoist user for years. It is my favorite note app but I also store other information I can share lists with others. The free app is also great & I can share with free users. Besides shopping lists, I use todoist to keep household I formation such as air filter sizes, paint colors, dimensions, etc. It syncs across all devices.

I used Apple Reminders for a while until I switched ecosystems. You have to pay to get the most out of it, but it's worth it to me. Excellent integration with Google Calendar and it works on all my platforms (Windows, Android, and Chromebook). Helps out a lot. Not the best voice assistant integration, but still great!

Prepare to pay. It looks like a very useful app if you pay for it. The basic features I have access to for free are good; easy to use & had a nice front end user experience. I can't give 5 stars because I'm not going to pay to access the extra features when I can use Samsung Notes for free for the same basic abilities. I don't need it bad enough to pay.

It's okay. I thought it would be much better for tracking recurring tasks. Once you miss a task, it just says overdue. Even if it happens multiple times a day, you won't be getting reminded again. Not worth paying for pro with notifications if this is how it works. I paid for a year but I won't be renewing if this doesn't change.

My experience with Todoist has been great. It has helped me keep track of all my college assignments that I must complete. It's also helpful that it reminds you when things are overdue. Making the list is very simple as well. All you do is press the + button, type the thing you need to do, press the > button, and BAM! you're all set.

I got lucky that this is the first to-do list app I have ever used. It is excellent in every way! It allows you to add items, prioritize items, move items around on your list, categorize items, and has multiple inboxes all with the free version. More options are available for the premium version if you need them.

Searching for the right Todo app has been a task (no pun intended) but I knew I found the right one when I stumbled across Todoist. This app is simply amazing! Talk about organization... You build Projects, then sub-projects, then daily routines, then work lists, the examples are endless... It's great. They days of "what else do I need to do" are gone! If your a business professional, or just a regular Joe looking for simple organization- Todoist is for you. Premium version is well worth the yearly subscription. Thanks Team Todoist!

Mostly a good app, helps me keep on top of things. Morning notifications are a bloody lifesaver. My only criticisms are that the free version is rather lacking in basic features, at least for people like me coming from other apps. Also the top bar and the system status bar when in the app are different colors, slightly, and that's always bugged me.

If you sign up for this app, understand you will received excessive emails, without the ability to actually unsubscribe. I have tried REPEATEDLY, yet still receive emails weekly or more. I just tried again, marked no to everything, only to receive a "tips and tricks" email literally one minute later. I will never reconsider using this app after this bs.

I really wanted to like this app but there is no way to order items on todo lists. I have at this point had to reorder all of my items over 10 times for each because when u use the drag and drop menu to change the order it doesn't save it permanently there is also no way to sort your todo lists by date because I figured if I input a deadline for each item it would show me what I had to do with the one due the soonest at the top but no. I wish there were more ways to sort or at least filter date

I adore this app. I type in everything I need to get done, then sort it all by date and priority. At the beginning of the day, I pick three tasks to rate as priority one. At the end of the day, I can easily postpone tasks I didn't accomplish without feeling bad. I feel organized, and like I can finally tackle my seemingly endless to-do list in a logical manner.

Still no way to bulk-clear completed tasks. You're also limited to 5 "projects" (Todoist's name for groups of tasks, like "Groceries", "To Do", etc.) Were you an early adopter that had more projects? Don't delete any of them, they'll be gone forever. This was my go-to note taking app for years. No longer.

This little app got me through my finals this quarter. The simplicity and variety of scheduling options makes it very useful. At first I thought premium was dumb and I wouldn't need it, but they offered a free 30 day trial without having to add my credit card and I quickly got addicted. Once the 30 day trial was over I couldn't go back so I paid the 36$ year which I would say is extremely reasonably priced. Very recommended, especially for school. Helped me stay on track and never miss any hmwk

My favorite to-do app out of the insane plethora of to-do apps. I tried a lot but this one stuck with me the most. The key for me was how simple it is but powerful at the same time. A quick "Water plants every other Saturday p3 #House" correctly identifies the list it belongs to, sets up the frequency and marks its priority. This app seriously puts me in control of my day so nothing "slips" my mind any more. I have recommended it to my boss and he also loves it.

Organized, takes a slight bit of getting used to, but highly recommended. Have gotten several people into using it & is effective to the degree that I may push for my company to embrace its use as well. DEVS: Can we get a slightly different visual indicator for the "Today" & "Inbox" screens? I often edit my own tasks & those I have collab'd on, & often catch myself forgetting what screen I'm in. Colored bar down one side, slightly tinted background to match project color, etc. KEEP IT UP! :)

Please get rid of the check mark boxes or give us an option to opt back to swiping. Other than that, I've been using and loving this app for years! It is exactly what I need as a college student, worker, mom, and just someone who likes keeping household tasks in order. I could only love this app and widget more if you got rid of those check mark boxes, please! I accidentally hit like 5 items in one day alone. thanks!

Perfect for organizing big life goals and daily tasks all in one. I've been using the pro version for 5 years now. I love how easy it is to organize tasks within one another and i use the scheduling features to their fullest extent. I use it on all my devices. Works well with small teams too. Now i never worry about losing an idea or missing a task. It's all written down forever and I never have to worry about what I forgot.

I love todoist.... I have stuck with this software longer than its competitors. I gave it 4 stars because I want more themes( nature, full dark mode themes, more colors like even soft pinks, lavender, powder blue) I would love an alarm on my tasks because reminders just dont catch my attention. I would love to be able to make list with checkboxes or numbers in comments on a task. I wish this app had a timer on each task as well. I love this app.

Great App! No complaint at all. I have friends who like using actual planners and notbooks to plan their day/week, but i decided to give this app a chance and paid for the annual subscription and it's really worth it. We can sometimes forget to have with us our planner but we never forget our phones and that's why this app has helped me a lot to get my tasks done. I trully recommend it (In its premium version though).

Best GTD app. If you are building a list system based on David Allen's book getting things done, I think this is absolutely the best app to use for that. I have had GTD systems on Evernote, Onenote, Microsoft to do, and a paper notebook, and this is by far the best in almost every way. It's easy to use, has no bugs, has a bunch of great bells and whistles, and it syncs seamlessly across all platforms. The user experience is great too. It draws me in to my lists, rather than repelling me.

One of the main sticking points for me is how well an app does recurring tasks. In particular, not just fixed date recurrences, but the ability to set the next date off the last completion date. So many apps fail to offer this. Alas, I could find NO way to do a recurring task at all, in spite of them saying it can be done. So, much less checking on this nuance, I see no way of doing them at all. Fail. Moving on.

After turning off *all* of the notifications, it's exactly the To Do app *I* need. Instead of sixty sheets of paper with projects I'm working on or was working on or ideas for art or groceries or whatever! I have this. I've been using it about a week, and so far, I think it's working out rather well.

I set multiple test reminders to see what kind of notification I'd receive and I haven't gotten a single one. I checked permissions and settings to make sure nothing turned off by default. I set a few at different times for reminders and they all passed by with nothing. No push or emails or anything. I can't exactly recommend using a reminder app that doesn't actually give you reminders.

Cannot use because of the 299 entry limit. My most important list was split into 7 lists. The first list segment is full, so I cannot add a new entry to the top of it. I copied my lists from Wunderlist, and several lists got split like that. If it had copied starting at the bottom of the list so the first segment was the one not full, I might have been able to make it work.

Found out about todoist from Jeff Su on YT (<- very infomative content on a variety of relevant subjects, look him up). The ease of access to be able to just throw a reminder into the phone with a few clicks and "close that tab" in your brain is priceless. I highly recommend it if you have a lot on your plate, get caught up in your work and lose track of time easily or just dont want to run around the house for a pen and paper when you're making a grocery list.

You have to pay for reminders, and I think that's stupid. Every single other app in the world sends me push notifications I DON'T want, and now, in the context of an app that keeps track of tasks you have to get done, notifications telling you to do those tasks are a premium service? Seriously, give me a break... I'll just use Google Keep Notes after all...

This app is a life saver! 🛟 I am happily neurodivergent because of ToDoist. I do, however, have a feature request: an alarm clock. Yes, I know every phone comes with an alarm clock, but not all alarm clocks are created equally. I trust the folks at ToDoist to develop a reliable alarm clock as an extension to their stupendous time management app. Please add an alarm clock, please! 🙏

This app is my lifeline but it's insanely buggy the past couple weeks, and keeps crashing. Always has had a problem syncing with browser extensions as well. Either browser or the app won't update globally. Usually it's the browser that won't but lately the app won't update and the browser extensions are the only ones working properly. Please help fix this todoist! All the typical troubleshooting is not helping. 2.14 edit back up to 5 stars - update fixed! Woohoo 🙌🎉

The Android client is convenient but unreliable. It often fails to sync edits (especially offline) and loses all my work, or outright fails to apply edits at all. The iOS client doesn't seem to have this issue though I only occasionally use it. Rarely the web client has serious offline sync issues and loses work. These issues with the Android client have been going on for years across multiple Pixel phones, so it is not device or version specific. I mostly treat android as a read-only client now

Mostly pros but one big con.. Absolutely love this app. It is really is user friendly and has helped me so much with organizing my life. One big con though is I had a big issue with the Google calendar integration. I had to disconnect it as it messed up my calendar and I lost a bunch of data that I needed to reenter. Luckily, it was uploaded to todoist so I didn't have to hunt for it. I really hope they fix that issue.

Very annoying app. Frequently pops annoying notices about made-up goals and badges while I'm trying to get actual work done. I'm not joking. I was trying to get things done and it pops up an award or badge for getting 5 things done and then tells me that it was 5 more than yesterday. In what world do I care about their stupid goals for me??? I have to waste my time reading the messages in case they are actually important.

Love this app! It's easy to use, and once you get used to it, it gets even easier. It's the first app I've ever actually stuck to, because of its goal setting. It gives "Karma" points for successfully marking things off, and makes me want to stick with it. I emailed the developers with a question just one time, and they were quick and helpful with their response. The Premium version is worth every penny!

Absolutely love this app because this is a digital version of how I've always organically done it. Can assign things to projects or labels. Have due dates and repeating events but also no dates if you want! Love how the website/desktop version is so similar to the app that navigating between the two is a breeze In terms of minor changes I'd like to be able to repeat but "every other day" or "every monday, Tuesday, and Thursday" for semi regular events/chores/tasks. Overall 10/10

Easy to use. There are all kinds of ways to organize your to do list. o like that I was able to set it up using the GTD method. I also like that Google calendar can be integrated and accessed through the app. A couple of frustrations...I had to try multiple times to set the due date and time for routine tasks. I also wish whole sections along with associated tasks could be moved to another project. I also wish you could pick multiple labels when creating a task instead of having to edit it later

Seems OK at creating shopping lists. But there are two massive failures: * reminders are weak. Once daily in a crowded notification panel is not a reminder. I want full screen "do this now", but that's not an option - despite explicity getting permissions to do exactly that. * reoccurring tasks are not reliable. Our cat gets insulin 2x a day. I bought the "pro" plan for two people so we don't duplicate doses, but the tasks just dissappeared after a couple days not marking it done. 🤦‍♂️

This app has great features and usability and it's awesome that it's cross-platform. however, one of the major issues that I've run into is that on my Android pixel devices, it seems to crash. every time I attempt to open it it starts to load and then immediately force crashes on its own. please fix for Android and I would once again highly rate this app.

I've been using Todoist for about a month. It is a great app that has helped me organise and track all the tasks. It is excellent for quickly capturing a thought: you just open a textbox and write, "Do something on Sep 1 at 16:00," and the app automatically sets up the reminder. If you couple it with Google Calendar, it creates a very efficient workflow. I also want to highlight the high quality of customer support. I had a minor issue, and they resolved it quickly.

I downloaded this a year ago and just found it a bit complicated. I have just been watching some videos, and am going to give it another try. So far, I have been unable to download a template recommended on your youtube channel, Eat the Frog, which seemed to fit my novice level, and that is the reason for only three stars. Finally managed to partly download Eat the Frog and can specify task so that the word "frog" appears but the icon does not. Update. Starting to appreciate app. Up a star.

It has been impossible for me to successfully schedule recurring tasks lately, and when I've managed to, the task recently disappeared after I completed the 3rd occurrence. I don't understand why this is so hard. The plain language description of how you want the event to occur is so frustrating. I kept saying every second day starting today, which was a Friday, but it always pushed it to Monday. When I tried to schedule the reminder it kept putting me at 2:00 a.m. Totally baffling.

Great app, has almost everything I could ask for in a task management app. But in the latest update, every single time I open the app it prompts to change the time zone to GMT-6 (what I already have it set as), even if I hit "never ask". Sometimes it even pops up multiple times while I'm using the app.

Almost perfect. I am now on my third day of using this app, and wow, it works. Chunking projects into "quests" and ticking them like in an RPG changes my life. There is only one quite big issue. Calendar sync. I have two calendars, one Outlook, one Google, and I cannot sync one to the other. In the app I can only sync one, but that isn't how it works for me, nor for a lot of people. Todoist should allow to have "secondary" calendars to import events and stuff to be shown in the app.

working fine on my laptop but won't open on my phone. tried uninstalling and reinstalling but it won't open. Adding on 3/14/26. My email address has changed and I can't seem to change it. So, using the website for help is no help at all. update...The instructions on how to edit my email address worked. then magically my phone app started working again. 3/17/26

Lots of bugs, creates my entries twice, deleting one of the duplicates deletes both. Sometimes stuff works, most of the times it doesn't. When there is an update, just keep the old version, as the update is most likely breaking stuff instead of adding useful features.

absolutely love this app! I was having difficulty getting all that I had to do each day organized and was getting distracted. This app has streamlined my day. I get much more accomplished everyday. The best part was there is virtually no learning curve. It's simple to set up and very intuitive.

I've tried just about every task management app out there, and Todoist is the best by far in terms of balancing functionality and ease of use. The standard version is great, but the Pro version is worth every cent for those who live on to-do lists. There's nothing the app can't do. I was bummed at first that it no longer integrates with Google Assistant, but that's no Todoist's fault. And honestly, Ramble is a million times better and understands natural language amazingly well.

So far so good. I've started using this alongside listening to the audiobook version of Redeeming Your Time by Jordan Raynor. It's helping me get all my open loops and craziness in my mind into projects and actions. Hopefully it keeps being useful.

The app is great for keeping track of things to do. Only drawback I experienced is that at one point it started to become gameified and giving you achievements and a lot of notifications. I turned EVERYTHING off, but still achievements even though goals were also off. Other than that I can recommend the app and it offers nice features like grouping the tasks etc.

As a serial procrastinator, this definitely helps to keep me on track. My productivity has increased dramatically. I just empty my brain of tasks and let Todoist keep track. I feel less pressure because I no longer worry as much about forgetting stuff (which I do alot). I have tunnel vision, and Todoist helps me to focus on one task at a time without losing sight of everything else. It's like my own full-time assistant. I keep it on all my devices so I can add tasks anywhere, anytime.

Had been great for years! Just what we needed. After the last update, the red "done" (send?) button does not work for the second task (or more) is added. First task works fine, the second fails to turn actionable even after text is entered. the user must force close the app to be able to enter any additional tasks, and can only add one before experiencing the same defect. We use this for grocery shopping where several tasks are added back to back and is borderline unusable now.

This has been great but just became unusable. The submit button shares space with the microphone / ramble button. But when app-switching away and returning, the microphone button no longer turns to a submit/send button when text is entered. The only way to solve this is to force close and reopen the app. I usually add items to my lists while referencing other apps on my phone. This bug prevents me from performing my primary use case, and renders this app unusable.

I really enjoy using Todoist, however, I do wish the phone widget would show connected calendar events in the "Today" and "Inbox" view, just the same as the full Todoist phone app does. I would save me from the need to pull my calendar each day I have appointments.

This app was always great, but after the last update I no longer get notifications for my reminders. I get the morning notification telling me I have X tasks that day, and the evening one telling me I have X left, but the notifications for the actual tasks have stopped coming through at the specified times. Please look into addressing this, as it renders the app useless.

Ironically, I've missed more tasks because of Todoist than I likely would have had I not used it. That's because my reminders are stuck in the app, and it won't notify me about them unless I pay or open the app. I rarely open the app. I suppose I should use a different app to remind me to look at my reminders in Todoist...

this is exceptional. the free version is absolutely wonderful and allows full organization. I was so impressed that I bought the pro version. for me this is essential to keeping organized. well done all!

As a uni student with ADHD, this app has helped me greatly with visualising what I need to get done, and remembering all the extra stuff I usually write in my notes app just to forget anyway. Even just the free version is an amazing tool. Major props to the developers <3

Using todoist as a replacement for Google Keep reminders after Keep got integrated with Calendar which was a train wreck for my needs. App is very simple to use and the reminders are well organized as a simple list that shows the tasks until you check it off as completed. there are short tutorial videos if needed.

good it helps you to build a schedule. and it is good. it helps in many ways. we can edit more. but i expect the notification "to do" if I was not finished the work. like it should push me to do the work.
So far I'm enjoying Todoist, but I really wish there was a more compact view of tasks especially for Today. As someone with ADHD who is very visual, I need to see more of my tasks at once.

bye bye ToDo. look i was a Wunderlist fan before Microsoft bought them. Big fan of David Allen and GTD. However, ToDo is falling behind the times!! Todoist has a great ai feature you can use that adds to your lists...using voice commands! and so much more. its the app I cant stop telling everyone about. Bravo Todoist team for creating a great team and culture! ✨️🙌🏽🙌🏽🙌🏽🙌🏽

I used to like Todoist a lot. Great interface, features, and run by solid people. BUT over my past several years of using on various devices, there's been a decline in the basic functioning of the app. This has been going on for awhile, and resulted in work productivity losses. EDIT: Customer service is great, but sync reliability is still inconsistent. Hopefully they prioritize improving this basic functionality before prioritizing new features.

The ad that brought me here boasted about the ai voice recognition but didn't mention the free tier is ruined by the connection to a voice recognition app with no free tier. My previous list app is free with no restrictions on its voice recognition so it's likely I'll just go back there.

You can do anything, just download this app and create your habits etc., create your tasks, it is not completely free but still it is the best, till now for me it is the best for you. I have no words to describe its greatness. JUST USE IT AND BECOME YOUR PRIME VERSION 😈😈😈.

Absolutely DO NOT recommend! I tested this, saw it was horribly messy UI and none of what I'd use and tried to delete my account. Well the app says NO! You have to create password to do so.. go to their website, but no.. horrible problems with google sync, it forces app open on mobile website and can't delete account. Must use other device, but no.. still the deletion is buggy and messy, and finally ends in blank white screen with no way of knowing. Can't possibly imagine this being helpful.

Good app, easy to create lists, especially if you remember a task or have a new idea out of the blue. It's just really sad that you need a premium account for basic notification reminder variety. What disappointed me the most wasnt crucial, tbh, but it did bother me personally, which is the app look. I was expecting it to look much more polished, but it feels like it uses a slight old-Android design language, which is even more obvious after the release of Material 3 Expressive.

great app, only real improvement I could do with is notes within each task. better than comments, somewhere to add notes about the task, the ability to add bulleted, numbered, tickable and even collapsible lists, for materials needed for the task.

Very good app with a simple to use and clean interface. Generally speaking the natural language does a good job of understanding the recurring tasks. One recent issue I've had is the widget just breaking and having to be deleted and readded. Worked fine for months but now it happens constantly.
After the last update my calendar items are no longer added to my to do list. I've been trying to fix it for an hour but I think they broke it. Time to uninstall and find an app that works.

I use it every single day both for work and personal if you find yourself needing a task app with a deep hiararchry, extreme customization, and ability to create teams or share live lists with others, this is it!

9/10 Legit- is a solid app. plays well with mobile n desktop. csv import + webhooks(OAuth) + automation(generous api) = better free tier features than most paid task managers. currently my hands free crm.
Great features even on the free version; very easy to use for personal, business, and other purposes

Todoist is a very modern, flexible, integrated, and powerful platform designed to wrangle the complexity of modern life into something satisfyingly workable. I am a former Remember The Milk Pro user of more than 15 years. Just a few days in, I wish I'd made the switch years ago. Inertia is a thing y'all!

yet another major update that breaks everything. every couple of years you push out some bad release just is just broken. this time it's the app doesn't work if you leave then return, you need to force close it. also pasting in multiple tasks at once has stopped working too

Been using Todoist every day for 10 years, always paid for the premium version. Then they do an update and it won't even open, and I'm locked out. Contacted support and they said yeah we'll keep you updated, then never heard back. Now I'm out money, lost all my access and I'm screwed. I'm sure they'll respond with their straight outta coms-school answers (where they use your first name, like we're pals) Ticket number 903851 and good timing sending me the price increase email. Garbage.

I've tried basically every to-do app and this is by the far the best. The natural language recognition is top tier and the Add Task widget/shortcut is awesome. Combined, I can unlock my phone and instantly enter a new to-do task without even thinking about it. E.g. 'take the bins out 6pm tomorrow' It recognises the date and time, and adds the reminder for me when I hit enter. All in all, takes 2 taps and about 5 seconds.
Fantastic app, my only complaint is I wish that the number of subtasks were shown in compact mode. I really need the number in my face to remind me I'm not finished.

the new item should be placed at the top of the section list. it's inconvenient having to scroll all the way down a long list every time. - it wastes so much time. if there is a setting for more smaller items & font size that allow to show more items in screen, it will be 5 star. plz don't ask me adjust my whole phone font size, there is only todoist font size too large only and other apps are okay.

I sure hope greed teaches you a lesson. $60 for an app subscription is disgusting. This app really helped with my disability, but I could hardly afford it when It was $50. What's the extra $10 for? I'll go buy some notepads instead.

Completing a repeating task late should not alter the following days time. There is no reasonable justification for choosing for me a time I set personally. I didn't choose the time for it to be dynamic.

I absolutely love this app! It helps me start by setting times for my tasks and offers so many features, even when offline. It reminds me of my tasks every day, which is super helpful. The app is also extremely organized, making it easy to manage everything efficiently.
very useful to plan and manage tasks. It's the best I ever used... it's like a Microsoft To-do combined to Trello

Great app and been using for years. I upgraded from legacy pro to pro 2 weeks ago and was enjoying but yesterday it reverted to basic even though play says I have the subscription (and money left account). Emailed todist support but no response in 24 hours which is actually hurting my productivity....

I used the app for a week and to be honest it's far from suitable. Task capture is fast and natural language works great. Kanban is not correct as tasks don't move to the done column on completion. The sync is completely broken on android which I discovered after a few days of work, as the web app was showing a completely different state. Manually syncing did nothing. This is a simple and fundamental feature that should not break so easily. I am now using obsidian with the TaskNotes plugin.

I really wanted an app that can help organize my multiple tasks, meetings, etc from both personal and work obligations. I can only link one email which is super unfortunate and doesn't meet my current needs. I am desperately hoping to find an app that will link Google email and multiple Microsoft office emails so I can combine all of my to-do list and meeting reminders into one place.

Very good. I like the fact you can set tasks to repeat, unlike other similar apps where I have to undelete to do the next day. there does appear to be a bug on Android though. when tabbing between apps with the task creation open, the app locks up and the only way out is to close the app.

when you add something in Arabic to English text, even just one character, the whole text gets flipped around, as if you're now reading the entire thing from right to left as with Arabic. Please fix this.

I've tried so many different checklist apps, but this one is the best of all of them. The free version is completely usable! Usually, they make you buy some sort of membership to do the smallest things, but not this one! All the options are simple and easy to understand, too. I don't have to take time to organize every single task, and it's just as easy to put tasks in.
The free version is too limited, feels like a limp trial version intended to get you to pay rather than a usable alternative. Lots of better apps with more robust free versions, laters gators.

Dear Lord, figuring out why sometimes the system cannot save a task, demanding to add context is impossible. Can't easily see the calendar view to understand a task load. Still trying to integrate this thing with Google calendar as it cannot be done easily.

I've been using this app for about 2 years now, and I have the widget on my home screen too to note quick updates and tasks. It's easy to use and very useful. I personally love the customisation options that this app provides.

100% recommend if youre someone who doenst need overcomplicated apps that has too many features and only want to list things to do. its easy to use, ive been using if for days since i downloaded it. the app features arent overwhelming, its a 10/10 use for me.

I love this app and use it constantly...lately, though, I notice difficulty in adding tasks that require me to close and restart the app. It only started yesterday, so it might be related to your recent update. Thanks.

The best productivity app I've ever used. It includes so many uncommon features that are just golden. Can be optimized and customized fully to run and maintain the systems of any CEO in the world. I've never expected so many features and little details, yet such an intuitive system.
Surprising easy to use. Just checking out the free version still. It links with my Google calendar so that's nice. It works just the YouTube videos I watched.
I love rambling and Customization. it works well during high energy sessions when I'm all over the place on 4 difference devices. adaptable, consistent .
easy to use. only get 10 rambles(audio notes) per month for free version. very easy to capture tasks and set the reminder time. not good for longer notes of text.

Update: Thanks for addressing this and rolling out a fix. Looking forward to it. Original: Since the lasted update the Widget seems to has glitches and does not load anymore! Could someone check on that please?!
this has been so life changing for me. I hope I can continue to use this app so well but it has all the tools some one needs to get so many goals and systems put it place!
This thing is Great! Whether the free or paid version its the first to do list app that works out of the box, and is reasonable!
simply the best. I've tried so many todo and task management apps but i always came back to this one. it just does what it needs to do, and very well.

I changed from 5 start to 3 because the ramble button is broken. When I type and save and then try another one the ramble button shows instead of the save button and I have to restart the app every time.
Can you please, PLEASE stop breaking the widget. It breaks on basically every single update. I otherwise really like Todoist... but this issue is non stop.
It's a really great app that checks up on you and your tasks. Only if you are disciplined, then u'll realize the significance of this app

absolute trash...continuously sends you emails about your to do list to "keep you engaged" Updsted Response: If you were concerned about cluttering peoples inboxes you wouldn't automatically send people daily updates to "check" or "create" a list. It would seem the concern is keeping people engaged through any means possible.

A very shallow and honest raw app for just basic tasks. It's like a notepad but with animations, you CANNOT have time duration on tasks or whey they start and end?? Even reminders need upgrade to premium, it seems just like to get some easy money done. Microsoft to do list and Google tasks offer way too much more without any payment whatsoever, do not waste your time

Used this app for years and loved it but the last several months, there have been relentless bugs in the app that haven't gone away even after reinstalling and clearing cache. my today page can't scroll to the bottom, adding a task from the today page disappears, and syncing between mobile and web/desktop version is messed up despite being logged in with an Internet connection and pushing the sync. it's honestly embarrassing and sad for such a simple app.

It would be great if the home screen widget could show connected calendar events along with tasks for the day / upcoming days. Also, unless I'm being dense, there doesn't seem to be a way to make an already created task a sub-task of another without dragging which is not always easy if the tasks aren't near each other in the list or if both have been found by searching it can't be done at all. This feels like an important change. 🙏🏼🙏🏼
Excellent, easier to manage, the interface is so customizable it's great, great widget as well

Trialling as replacement for Ultimate To Do list which is great but out of support and will no longer sync across devices. Had high hopes for this one but major drawback for me is the recurring daily tasks that skip days if you complete when overdue eg if daily task due Mon and completed on Tues I want the next recurrence to be on Tues not Weds, eg it would be great to have the option to recur based on either completed date or next calculated date from due date based on desired recurrence

I loved this app so much I stuck with it for over 10 years. I paid for it because I got value and enjoyed the new features. Today I got an email telling me I can stay on my current payment plan and get nothing new or pay double for AI I don't want. Instantly reducing the value of my annual plan I'm less than half way through. I'm so sick of AI everything and ramp up the price. I'm now done and will go put my money on another upcoming developer instead since loyal users here are less valued.

I tried a bunch of planners, about a dozen, all in one evening, super thrilled when I found this from a YouTube recommendation, uninstalled the others immediately. The layout and features in the free version are really helpful in organising my tasks, I would get the paid version when I can afford it. I wish it has a one time life fee though.

Seems like it would be great if it wasn't so buggy. On date picker, it saves the date before the one you've chosen. Crashed on me a couple other times when trying to save date. Tried the moonstone theme and now it won't change to any other ones.
Was wondering about the app at first but I'm now a believer! Fantastic App! Very Helpful Good Developer no bugs. 5 stars.

As a person with ADHD, this app has been genuinely life changing. So thank you for that. One personal suggestion I'd like to make to the team is to add a point system, even if it's only as a paid feature. Tasks with more priority should give out more points compared to less important tasks. A daily goal system based on such points instead of no. of tasks could be a more accurate representation of what we've accomplished in our day. Other than that, the app is just perfect.
Somewhat intuitive to set up. Told not to send alert emails. Continued to. Deleted.

No afs, simple and straightforward. Amazing and can't rrccomend it enough for simple todos and personal organization. You can also invite others for collab organization, but ad an individual to do app I love it since it easily syncs with my devices without needing extra fluff. Android version also has really good widgets!

Very helpful and easy to use. However, I wished it had a feature that allows you to pick certain apps and it will just make task suggestions based on an AI overview. It would make it easy for me to also manage the amount of time I spend doing certain work and hobbies.

After the latest update, the app just crashes right after launch. I don't know what's wrong with the app developers nowadays. Why is that so hard to roll out a new update without breaking the app completely? This is especially awful because I use it every day and I'm a paid subscriber.

It's the best app for maintaining the todo list for looking for a subscription free app. It's not completely subscription free however all the free features are good enough to manage the todo list. Subscription just adds the cherry on the cake.

I've been jumping between scheduling apps for years, never satisfied with any of them. This is the first one I try that I'm going to stick to. Make sure to add this app's schedule as a widget on your phone so you don't need to click the app to see your schedule
Super easy to use app. It's nice to have when you have to decide a schedule on your phone.
last version is bugged and can't add tasks anymore - there is just weird "snake" icon but not button to save
They force you to sign up so they can use your data. Avoid. Reply: It should be optional. Your comment added nothing of value.
can't choose date format. moved to sri lanka. todoist decide it's mmddyy now. upd advice from developers sounds good, doesn't work. web page and pc app all date settings are ddmmyy

Brilliant app! It helps to ensure my jobs as a Specialist Kitchen Hand (SKH) at Hungry Jack's are always completed on time, and it helps me to stay on top of chores at home. On the topic of mental health, I have an idea to help expand your app further and transform it from just a To Do List app, to a mentally equipped chore app. If you can add a feature where we can assign "breaks," that'll help to motivate us with ADHD. Even time before the next upcoming task can automatically become breaks.

so far I'm really enjoying this app. one of the best features is that you can just ramble all the things you need to do and it creates a really cool little to-do list, with the date when you need to do it. the task then appears in your today list on the day you had it planned. there's also a project planner, which is brilliant! in the free version you can create 5 projects, you can also create templates and duplicate the project. these are really useful for when I have intakes for my clients.
This app does not work properly in Hindi language, tax is not selected when writing Hindi characters in it and it is also not in Hindi language I am from Asia so it is a Indian

I loved this app, but you almost doubled the price this year for no significant upgrade. I only upgraded so I could have more projects, so I'm switching to another free app that lets me have more. Canceled my subscription.

New to this product, so still evaluating, but it seems solid. Disappointed it doesn't have a concept of "Start Date", but this seems rare in To Do apps, to my utter dismay. Used Toodledo for years for that reason and that it's very customizable, but its UI is very old and tired and there doesn't appear to be anything in the roadmap to update. Plus, the Android app is currently broken for me with no acknowledgment from Toodledo's developers of the problem, so I'm in search of a new solution.
